learning_outcomes.description: Describe the functions of various endocrine systems including the major organs involved and the disease states they may regulate
Effectively explain the structure and function of the central nervous system and the various pathologies associated with altered brain function
Apply knowledge of the physiological processes that underpin learning and memory to explain disorders associated with their alteration
Describe structures associated with the special senses to describe how they function in health and disease
Understand the anatomy and physiology of the integumentary system and how it is impacted by burns and the process of wound healing
Apply Knowledge of the anatomy and physiology of the cardiovascular system to understand pathogenesis of cardiovascular disorders
Describe the functions of the immune system and how it is involved in infection and disease
Describe the main modes of actions of drugs and their clinical uses in patients with nervous system disorders, endocrine disorders, cardiovascular disease, immune disorders and cancer
Effectively communicate how a disruption of homeostasis can lead to an acute illness, chronic health disorder or death
Apply basic observational and experimental techniques in human physiology
Be able to explain how various physiological systems integrate in health and disease

assessments.task_description:
The in-semester examination will cover the content of lectures delivered in weeks 1 to 4 inclusive.

The exam will be a closed book invigilated exam held on campus, undertaken via the Inspera eAssessment platform. Students will be required to bring a laptop to the exam that meets the device requirements for Inspera. It is important that you check before the exam that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library. The library website Get familiar with Inspera provides the latest information for students about using Inspera. Further information about the exam will be provided on Blackboard. 

The exam will be a closed book on-campus exam. 

Have your UQ student ID card available for all your exams. 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date: These 5 quizzes will be completed in class as part of the 2 prac classes and 3 workshops. See the timetable for your scheduled class times.
assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Quiz
assessments.task_description:
In each of the two practical classes and three workshop classes, you will be expected to complete an in‑class quiz. These quizzes may take different forms depending on the nature of the practical class or workshop. Each quiz will be assessed on a pass/fail basis. If you achieve 50% or more, you will be awarded full marks. If you achieve less than 50%, you will be awarded 0 marks.

You will receive a total score out of 20%, based on your best four scores from the five quizzes (5% per quiz, to a maximum of 20%).

Further assessment details will be provided on Blackboard during the semester.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Fact Sheet on one disruption of homeostasis
assessments.task_description:
Select ONE disruption of homeostasis in areas relevant to this course and create a Fact Sheet. Submission of this assessment is required to be submitted in both PDF and Word format through the Turnitin Link in Blackboard. 

Further assessment details will be provided on Blackboard during the semester.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit your Assessment task, with the electronic coversheet available on the School's website (under Undergraduate - Assessment), to Turnitin by the submission deadline. You may submit drafts to Turnitin up to the due date, but you will only be able to submit once on or after the due date. You should also keep an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit. School website
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This is a closed book examination. This examination must be completed individually. You are NOT permitted to share this exam with any un-authorised person. You must NOT make a copy of any of the material in the exam by any means.

The end of semester exam will cover material from weeks 5 to 13 (inclusive). 

The exam will be a closed book invigilated exam held on campus, undertaken via the Inspera eAssessment platform. Students will be required to bring a laptop to the exam that meets the device requirements for Inspera. It is important that you check before the exam that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library. The library website Get familiar with Inspera provides the latest information for students about using Inspera. Further information about the exam will be provided on Blackboard. 

The exam will be an on-campus exam. 

Have your UQ student ID card available for all your exams.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 4(23 Feb - 22 Mar)
learning_activities.topic:
Lecture series- Endocrinology in health and disease

Module 1:Endocrinology and metabolism in health and disease- James Cuffe (7 lecture hours) and Will Olsen (2 lecture hours)

In this module you will learn about how the endocrine system integrates physiological systems and controls systemic homeostasis. You will learn about key endocrine organs and systems, glucose homeostasis, nutrition and metabolism, endocrine control of growth, sex steroids, endocrine control of bone health and therapeutic approaches to manage endocrine disorders.


Learning outcomes: L04, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_title: Lecture series- Endocrinology in health and disease
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture series- Endocrinology in health and disease Module 1:Endocrinology and metabolism in health and disease- James Cuffe (7 lecture hours) and Will Olsen (2 lecture hours) In this module you will learn about how the endocrine system integrates physiological systems and controls systemic homeostasis. You will learn about key endocrine organs and systems, glucose homeostasis, nutrition and metabolism, endocrine control of growth, sex steroids, endocrine control of bone health and therapeutic approaches to manage endocrine disorders.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to the course [Cuffe]

Please make sure to attend the online introductory lecture delivered at 8am on the first day of semester. This lecture will explain how the course works and go through the complex timetable. This is a great chance for you to ask questions about how the course will run. The first content lecture will follow immediately also as an online lecture.


Learning outcomes: L01

This lecture will explain how the course works and go through the complex timetable. This is a great chance for you to ask questions about how the course will run. The first content lecture will follow immediately also as an online lecture.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 8(16 Mar - 26 Apr)
learning_activities.topic:
Lecture series- Nervous system and related disorders

Module 2: Nervous system and related disorders- Andrew Tosolini, (2 lecture hours), Mary-Louise Roy-Manchadi ( 5 lecture hours) and Will Olsen (1 lecture hour)

In this module you will expand your understanding of the the anatomy and physiology of the nervous system and take a disease focused approach to learn about the biological processes responsible for neuopathologies. This module will also include an overview of key medications and how they work to manage these disorders. Note this module starts in week 5 but does not finish until week 11


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L06, L07
learning_activities.topic_title: Lecture series- Nervous system and related disorders

This module will also include an overview of key medications and how they work to manage these disorders. Note this module starts in week 5 but does not finish until week 11

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 5(16 Mar - 29 Mar)
learning_activities.topic:
Oral glucose tolerance test [Cuffe]

There are multiple sessions for this class. Make sure to attend your allocated session.

In this practical class, students will fast overnight before assessing their blood glucose following a glucose load in what is a modified version of the clinically diagnostic test for diabetes mellitus. Students will be divided into two groups so check the timetable to determine which session you need to attend.


Learning outcomes: L10
learning_activities.topic_description: Oral glucose tolerance test [Cuffe] There are multiple sessions for this class. Make sure to attend your allocated session. In this practical class, students will fast overnight before assessing their blood glucose following a glucose load in what is a modified version of the clinically diagnostic test for diabetes mellitus. Students will be divided into two groups so check the timetable to determine which session you need to attend.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 5 To Week 6(23 Mar - 05 Apr)
learning_activities.topic:
Neuroanatomy practical [Tosolini]

There are multiple sessions for this class. Make sure to attend your allocated session.

In this practical class, students will enter the gross anatomy facility and extend their knowledge of the anatomy of the nervous system. This practical class will focus on a range of pathological specimens related to injuries and disorders of the brain. Students will be divided into two groups so check the timetable to determine which session you need to attend.


Learning outcomes: L09, L10
learning_activities.topic_description: Neuroanatomy practical [Tosolini] There are multiple sessions for this class. Make sure to attend your allocated session. In this practical class, students will enter the gross anatomy facility and extend their knowledge of the anatomy of the nervous system. This practical class will focus on a range of pathological specimens related to injuries and disorders of the brain. Students will be divided into two groups so check the timetable to determine which session you need to attend.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 6 To Week 9(30 Mar - 03 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Lecture series: Cardiovascular disease, immune system and cancer

Module 3: Cardiovascular disease, immune system and cancer- Jacky Suen (2 lecture hours), Lachlan Rash ( 4 lecture hours) and Esther Lau (2 lecture hours)

In this module you will build on you knowledge of the cardiovascular system and focus on the pathological processes involved in cardiovascular diseases. You will learn about how the immune system functions and learn about immune disorders and treatments. This will link to an introduction to cancer and its therapeutic management.


Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_title: Lecture series: Cardiovascular disease, immune system and cancer
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture series: Cardiovascular disease, immune system and cancer Module 3: Cardiovascular disease, immune system and cancer- Jacky Suen (2 lecture hours), Lachlan Rash ( 4 lecture hours) and Esther Lau (2 lecture hours) In this module you will build on you knowledge of the cardiovascular system and focus on the pathological processes involved in cardiovascular diseases. You will learn about how the immune system functions and learn about immune disorders and treatments. This will link to an introduction to cancer and its therapeutic management.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop: Heart Failure workshop [Suen]

There are multiple sessions for this class. Make sure to attend your allocated session.

In this workshop you will learn about the symptoms of Heart Failure and explore other conditions that may cause similar symptoms. In addition, you will learn about commonly used diagnostic tests available in hospitals, and their use for HF patients. Finally, we will discuss current available treatments. 

Learning outcomes: L09, L10
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop: Heart Failure workshop [Suen] There are multiple sessions for this class. Make sure to attend your allocated session. In this workshop you will learn about the symptoms of Heart Failure and explore other conditions that may cause similar symptoms. In addition, you will learn about commonly used diagnostic tests available in hospitals, and their use for HF patients. Finally, we will discuss current available treatments.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 9 To Week 13(27 Apr - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Lecture series: Integumentary system and system integration

Module 4: Integumentary system and system integration- Louise Ainscough (2 lecture hours), Lachlan Rash ( 1 lecture hour) and Mary-Louise Roy- Manchadi (3 lecture hours)

In this module you will learn about the structures and function of the skin and about how injuries are repaired (Note these lectures are recorded and can be studied at any time but will be assessed on the final exam). The rest of this module focuses on pathologies/disorders which involve multiple systems such as MODS, spinal cord injury and drug addiction.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06, L07, L08, L11
learning_activities.topic_title: Lecture series: Integumentary system and system integration

The rest of this module focuses on pathologies/disorders which involve multiple systems such as MODS, spinal cord injury and drug addiction.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop: Movement disorder workshop [Roy-Manchadi]

There are multiple sessions for this class. Make sure to attend your allocated session.

In this workshops students will learn about real world experiences of people with movement disorders.

Learning outcomes: L09, L10
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop: Movement disorder workshop [Roy-Manchadi] There are multiple sessions for this class. Make sure to attend your allocated session. In this workshops students will learn about real world experiences of people with movement disorders.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop: Cancer Therapeutics [Isaacs]

There are multiple sessions for this class. Make sure to attend your allocated session.

In this workshop you will learn about the various forms of cancer, how these cancers may be able to be treated and the side effects of cancer treatment. Students will be divided into two groups so check the timetable to determine which session you need to attend.

Learning outcomes: L10
